title: ¿Qué Dijo Realmente?
description: Identifica la forma completa de cada reducción. Decodifica el habla natural.

questions.question: You hear: "I'm gonna go." What is the full form?
questions.explanation: gonna = going to. "I'm gonna go" = "I am going to go."

questions.question: Informal: "I ___ leave now." (= I have got to leave now)
questions.explanation: gotta = got to / have got to. Expresa obligación informal.

questions.question: Which is appropriate for a JOB INTERVIEW?
options: "I would like to discuss my experience."
questions.explanation: Job interviews require formal language. "I would like to..." is the appropriate choice.

questions.question: You hear: "I dunno what to do." "Dunno" means...
questions.explanation: dunno = don't know. Very common in casual speech.

questions.question: "___ see that movie!" (= Let me see that movie!)
questions.explanation: lemme = let me. Used in very casual speech.

questions.question: "I'm kinda hungry." What does "kinda" mean?
questions.explanation: kinda = kind of = algo, un poco. "I'm kinda hungry" = "Tengo algo de hambre."

questions.question: "She hafta go now." "Hafta" is the reduction of...
"I ___ a pizza, please." (= I want to have a pizza — casual)
questions.explanation: wanna = want to. "I wanna a pizza" = "Quiero una pizza" (informal).

questions.question: In "turn it off", how does this sound in connected speech?
questions.explanation: Linking: the words connect smoothly. "turn it off" → "tur-ni-toff".

questions.question: "Gimme a break!" What is the full form?
questions.explanation: gimme = give me. Very casual/informal expression.

title: Forma Reducida → Forma Completa
description: Empareja cada reducción con su equivalente completo.

title: Reducciones Esenciales
description: Aprende cada reducción con su forma completa, regla y contexto de uso.

cards.back: = going to. Solo habla informal y casual. NUNCA en escritura formal.
cards.example: I'm gonna call you later. (= I'm going to call you later.)

cards.back: = want to. Informal. Se usa con "I", "you", "we", "they". NO con "he/she/it".
cards.example: I wanna go home. (= I want to go home.)

cards.back: = got to / have got to. Obligación informal. Muy común en EE.UU.
cards.example: I gotta leave now. (= I have got to leave now.)

cards.back: = have to. Obligación informal. Alternativa a "gotta".
cards.example: You hafta try this restaurant. (= You have to try this restaurant.)

cards.back: = let me. Muy casual. Petición informal.
cards.example: Lemme think about it. (= Let me think about it.)

cards.back: = kind of. Significa "algo, un poco". Suaviza lo que dices.
cards.example: I'm kinda tired. (= I'm kind of tired.)

cards.back: = don't know. Muy informal. A veces se escribe 'I dunno'.
cards.example: I dunno what happened. (= I don't know what happened.)

cards.back: = give me. Muy casual/informal. Puede sonar demandante.
cards.example: Gimme a break! (= Give me a break!)

cards.back: = a lot of. Informal. Cantidad grande.
cards.example: There are a lotta people here. (= There are a lot of people here.)

cards.back: = don't you. Assimilation: la /t/ + /y/ se fusionan en /tʃ/ ("ch").
cards.example: Don'tchu think so? (= Don't you think so?)

title: De Formal a Natural
description: Reconstruye la oración usando contracciones y reducciones naturales.

sentences.prompt: Make natural: "I do not know what I am going to do."
sentences.correct: I dunno what I'm gonna do.

sentences.prompt: Make natural: "I want to go to the movies."
sentences.correct: I wanna go to the movies.

sentences.prompt: Make natural: "I have got to leave right now."
Make natural: "Let me see what I can do."
sentences.correct: Lemme see what I can do.

sentences.prompt: Make natural: "She is kind of busy today."
Make natural: "Give me a minute, I am going to help you."
sentences.correct: Gimme a minute, I'm gonna help you.

sentences.prompt: Make natural: "You have to try this restaurant."
Make natural: "I do not know, I want to think about it."
sentences.correct: I dunno, I wanna think about it.

title: El Espectro de Formalidad
description: Explora el espectro de formal a informal y descubre cómo cambia el lenguaje.
